subtitle: Review task groupings, hand-offs, and readiness checks before implementation starts from them.

sections.title: Task Groups at a Glance
sections.label: Task Group
sections.why: Lead with the grouped task shape rather than isolated individual tasks.

sections.title: Dependency Chains and Hand-Offs
sections.label: Dependency
sections.why: Use a matrix when sequencing and hand-offs need side-by-side review.

sections.title: Readiness Checks and Acceptance Handholds
sections.label: Readiness Check
sections.why: Frame acceptance handholds as explicit implementation checks.

sections.title: Decisions Required for Approval
sections.label: Decision Gate
sections.why: Keep task-level approval calls dense and direct.

sections.title: Ambiguous Boundaries and Drift Risks
sections.label: Drift Risk
sections.why: Keep boundary ambiguity and drift risk visible before coding starts.

sections.title: Traceability Map
sections.label: Evidence
sections.why: Tie substantive approval claims to exact canonical evidence.

sections.title: Validator Status
sections.label: Validation
sections.why: Show canonical and approval-view validation status together.

sections.title: Downstream Impact if Approved
sections.label: Impact
sections.why: Clarify what downstream work may now treat as fixed.

glance_cards.label: Decision gates
glance_cards.hint: Approvals needed before task execution starts.

glance_cards.label: Drift risks
glance_cards.hint: Boundary ambiguity or drift pressure points.

glance_cards.label: Evidence claims
glance_cards.hint: Claims backed by exact task quotes.
